testcase.name: test_get_env_vars

failure.message: ValueError: ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one

testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "ORGANIZATION": "org", "REPOSITORY": "repo,repo2", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GH_TOKEN": "token", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "START_DATE": "2022-01-01", "END_DATE": "2022-12-31", "SPONSOR_INFO": "False", "LINK_TO_PROFILE": "True", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars(self): """ Test the get_env_vars function when all environment variables are set correctly. """
( organization, repository_list, gh_app_id, gh_app_installation_id, gh_app_private_key, gh_app_enterprise_only, token, ghe, start_date, end_date, sponsor_info, link_to_profile, > ) = env.get_env_vars() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env.py:68: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
test = False
def get_env_vars( test: bool = False, ) -> tuple[ str | None, list[str], int | None, int | None, bytes, bool, str, str, str, str, bool, bool, ]: """ Get the environment variables for use in the action.
Args: None
Returns: organization (str): the organization to get contributor information for repository_list (list[str]): A list of the repositories to get contributor information for gh_app_id (int | None): The GitHub App ID to use for authentication gh_app_installation_id (int | None): The GitHub App Installation ID to use for authentication gh_app_private_key_bytes (bytes): The GitHub App Private Key as bytes to use for authentication gh_app_enterprise_only (bool): Set this to true if the GH APP is created on GHE and needs to communicate with GHE api only token (str): The GitHub token to use for authentication ghe (str): The GitHub Enterprise URL to use for authentication start_date (str): The start date to get contributor information from end_date (str): The end date to get contributor information to. sponsor_info (str): Whether to get sponsor information on the contributor link_to_profile (str): Whether to link username to Github profile in markdown output """
organization = settings.get("ORGANIZATION") repositories_str = settings.get("REPOSITORY") # Either organization or repository must be set if not organization and not repositories_str: > raise ValueError( "ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one" ) E ValueError: ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one
env.py:113: ValueError

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_auth_with_github_app_installation_missing_inputs

failure.message: AssertionError: 'ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment v[33 chars] one' != 'GH_APP_ID set and GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID [34 chars] set' - ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one + GH_APP_ID set and GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID or GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY variable not set

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_invalid_start_date

failure.message: AssertionError: 'ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one' != 'START_DATE environment variable not in the format YYYY-MM-DD' - ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one + START_DATE environment variable not in the format YYYY-MM-DD

testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_invalid_start_date>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "ORGANIZATION": "org", "REPOSITORY": "repo,repo2", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GH_TOKEN": "token", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "START_DATE": "2022/01/01", "END_DATE": "2022-12-31", "SPONSOR_INFO": "False", "LINK_TO_PROFILE": "True", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars_invalid_start_date(self): """ Test the get_env_vars function when invalid start date given. Expect a ValueError to be raised. """
with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm: env.get_env_vars() the_exception = cm.exception > self.assertEqual( str(the_exception), "START_DATE environment variable not in the format YYYY-MM-DD", ) E AssertionError: 'ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one' != 'START_DATE environment variable not in the format YYYY-MM-DD' E - ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one E + START_DATE environment variable not in the format YYYY-MM-DD
test_env.py:137: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_missing_values

failure.message: AssertionError: 'ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one' != 'GH_TOKEN environment variable not set' - ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one + GH_TOKEN environment variable not set

testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_missing_values>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "ORGANIZATION": "org", "REPOSITORY": "repo,repo2", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GH_TOKEN": "", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "START_DATE": "2022-01-01", "END_DATE": "2022-12-31", "SPONSOR_INFO": "False", "LINK_TO_PROFILE": "True", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars_missing_values(self): """ Test the get_env_vars function when none of the environment variables are set. Expect a ValueError to be raised. """
with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as cm: env.get_env_vars() the_exception = cm.exception > self.assertEqual(str(the_exception), "GH_TOKEN environment variable not set") E AssertionError: 'ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one' != 'GH_TOKEN environment variable not set' E - ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one E + GH_TOKEN environment variable not set
test_env.py:109: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_no_dates

failure.message: ValueError: ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one

testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_no_dates>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "ORGANIZATION": "org", "REPOSITORY": "repo,repo2", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GH_TOKEN": "token", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "START_DATE": "", "END_DATE": "", "SPONSOR_INFO": "False", "LINK_TO_PROFILE": "True", }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars_no_dates(self): """ Test the get_env_vars function when all environment variables are set correctly and start_date and end_date are not set. """
( organization, repository_list, gh_app_id, gh_app_installation_id, gh_app_private_key, gh_app_enterprise_only, token, ghe, start_date, end_date, sponsor_info, link_to_profile, > ) = env.get_env_vars() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env.py:178: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
test = False
def get_env_vars( test: bool = False, ) -> tuple[ str | None, list[str], int | None, int | None, bytes, bool, str, str, str, str, bool, bool, ]: """ Get the environment variables for use in the action.
Args: None
Returns: organization (str): the organization to get contributor information for repository_list (list[str]): A list of the repositories to get contributor information for gh_app_id (int | None): The GitHub App ID to use for authentication gh_app_installation_id (int | None): The GitHub App Installation ID to use for authentication gh_app_private_key_bytes (bytes): The GitHub App Private Key as bytes to use for authentication gh_app_enterprise_only (bool): Set this to true if the GH APP is created on GHE and needs to communicate with GHE api only token (str): The GitHub token to use for authentication ghe (str): The GitHub Enterprise URL to use for authentication start_date (str): The start date to get contributor information from end_date (str): The end date to get contributor information to. sponsor_info (str): Whether to get sponsor information on the contributor link_to_profile (str): Whether to link username to Github profile in markdown output """
organization = settings.get("ORGANIZATION") repositories_str = settings.get("REPOSITORY") # Either organization or repository must be set if not organization and not repositories_str: > raise ValueError( "ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one" ) E ValueError: ORGANIZATION and REPOSITORY environment variables were not set. Please set one
env.py:113: ValueError

testcase.name: test_get_bool_env_var_that_exists_and_is_true
